title: Supply Chain Document Automation for Logistics Companies
metaTitle: Supply Chain Document Automation - Automate Logistics Workflows
metaDescription: Automate bills of lading, customs forms, and shipping manifests. Convert supply chain PDFs to Excel with AI for better logistics visibility.
h1: Supply Chain Document Automation for Manufacturers and Logistics Companies

hero.headline: Automate Bills of Lading, Customs Forms, and Shipping Manifests
hero.subheadline: Convert supply chain documents from PDF to structured Excel spreadsheets using AI. Improve logistics visibility and reduce manual data entry for manufacturers and freight companies.

summary: Supply chain document automation helps manufacturers and logistics companies extract data from bills of lading, customs forms, shipping manifests, and purchase orders. By converting these PDFs and images to structured Excel files, companies can track shipments, update inventory systems, and generate compliance reports more efficiently.
whoItsFor: Freight forwarders processing hundreds of bills of lading weekly
Manufacturing companies tracking purchase orders and shipping documents
Logistics coordinators managing customs paperwork and compliance forms
whenThisIsRelevant: Processing daily shipment manifests from multiple carriers
Extracting purchase order data for inventory management systems
Converting customs forms for regulatory compliance reporting
supportedInputs: Scanned bills of lading from shipping companies
Digital customs forms and commercial invoices
Purchase order PDFs from suppliers and vendors
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheets with shipment tracking numbers, dates, and destinations
CSV files containing purchase order details, quantities, and delivery schedules
commonChallenges: Manual entry of shipping manifest data takes hours per batch
Purchase order information scattered across different PDF formats
Customs forms require precise data extraction for compliance audits
Tracking inventory levels requires data from multiple document types
howItWorksSteps: Upload bills of lading, customs forms, or shipping manifests as PDFs or images
Select specific fields like tracking numbers, shipment dates, consignee details, and cargo descriptions
AI extracts the data and creates structured Excel files with one row per document
Download organized spreadsheets for import into ERP, WMS, or tracking systems
whyPdfExcelAiFits: Handles both digital PDFs and scanned shipping documents with OCR technology
Batch processes multiple documents from different carriers and formats simultaneously
Custom field selection lets you extract specific supply chain data points needed
Pipeline automation processes recurring shipment documents without manual intervention
limitations: Accuracy depends on document quality - poor scans or faded shipping labels may need manual review
Complex multi-page manifests with nested cargo details might require field customization
Handwritten notes on bills of lading have limited recognition compared to printed text

faq.question: Can this process bills of lading from different shipping companies?
faq.answer: Yes, the AI adapts to various bill of lading formats from different carriers. You may need to customize field selections for non-standard layouts, but it handles most common shipping document formats automatically.

faq.question: How accurate is the extraction for customs forms and commercial invoices?
faq.answer: Accuracy is 99%+ on clear, typed documents like digital customs forms. Scanned documents depend on scan quality, and complex multi-page invoices may need manual review of extracted data.

faq.question: Can I automate processing of daily shipment manifests?
faq.answer: Yes, pipeline automation can watch folders for new manifests and automatically process them. This works well for recurring document workflows where carriers send manifests in consistent formats.

faq.question: What supply chain data fields can be extracted from shipping documents?
faq.answer: Common fields include tracking numbers, shipment dates, origin/destination, consignee details, cargo descriptions, weights, and container numbers. You can select custom fields based on your specific logistics needs.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing for supply chain data security
99%+ accuracy on clear shipping documents and customs forms
Batch processing handles large volumes of logistics documents efficiently
Free tier available to test with your actual supply chain documents
exampleUseCases: Freight company extracts tracking data from 200+ daily bills of lading for customer portal updates
Manufacturer processes purchase orders from suppliers to update inventory management system
Logistics coordinator converts customs forms to Excel for quarterly compliance reporting
Distribution center automates shipping manifest data entry for warehouse management system
